\noindent Declaring the independent variables in a
different order shows that the calculation of the
sum of squares is in sequential order.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
ussteamco_mod_1b = fit_lm('ussteamco_mod_1b', 'revenue ~ 
heatDD + coolDD + production', USSteamCoEstim)
anova_mod_1b = anova_lm(ussteamco_mod_1b, typ=1)
print(anova_mod_1b)
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
